mission.title: spire: [RFC] Registrations RBAC
mission.description: [RFC] Registrations RBAC. This issue affects 3+ users.

steps.title: Identify spire feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your spire deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=spire
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: [RFC] Registrations RBAC

steps.title: Check current Namespace configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire resources:
```bash
kubectl get namespace -A
kubectl describe namespace <name> -n <namespace>
```

steps.title: Confirm [RFC] Registrations RBAC is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: **Pull Request check list**

**Affected functionality**

This PR adds a field to the middleware `Preprocess` function allowing passing of the request object for Unary gRPC calls. This will allow future enhancement to make more granular actions for `Preprocess` functions, i.e. for authorization to have finer grained policies (#1975) based on the body of the request. 

**Description of change**

Add. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
